channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Tech leads first half stock gains — but the biggest winners weren't in the U.S.
item.description: U.S. Big Tech stocks enjoyed strong gains — despite a sharp sell-off at the end of June — but were largely outperformed by their international counterparts.

item.title: Fed Chief Kevin Warsh set to give view on rates, central bank overhaul at ECB forum: Live updates
item.description: The Fed chairman joins a panel of other central bank leaders Wednesday at the ECB Forum on Central Banking in Sintra, Portugal.

item.title: Private payrolls rose by 98,000 in June, less than expected, ADP reports
item.description: Companies added slightly fewer workers than forecast, with hiring targeted heavily toward healthcare-related sectors.

item.title: Oil prices wobble as uncertainty persists over U.S.-Iran talks in Qatar
item.description: Oil prices were lower on Wednesday after Iran said it would not meet with U.S. delegates for talks in Qatar, amplifying concern about the peace process.

item.title: Grindr is launching two products that make the stock attractive, says Morgan Stanley
item.description: Investors should scoop up LGBT-focused dating app Grindr as the company rolls out two new products that can drive shares higher, according to Morgan Stanley.

item.title: Bulls are betting big on this global ETF that's deep in a bear market
item.description: U.S. stocks just keep winning, with the Nasdaq closing out its best quarter since 2020. Across the globe in China, things couldn't be more different.

item.title: Kroger to buy retailer Giant Eagle in a $1.65 billion deal
item.description: Kroger said on Wednesday it would buy supermarket chain Giant Eagle ⁠in a $1.65 billion deal, as it looks to scale amid intense ​competition.

item.title: Anthropic says Trump admin has lifted export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5
item.description: The U.S. Department of Commerce lifted the export controls on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models

item.title: Lamborghini reveals new Urus performance hybrid SUV after ditching EVs
item.description: Lamborghini is calling the new Urus SE Performante the "fastest Super SUV in the world."

item.title: Stocks making the biggest moves premarket: Nike, ServiceNow, Constellation Brands & more
item.description: These are the stocks posting the largest moves premarket.

item.title: Employers who laid off workers citing AI are already starting to regret it
item.description: Companies are realizing artificial intelligence can't do everything after all, prompting them to rehire employees to grow their businesses

item.title: Trump's annual financial disclosure shows more than $580M in crypto-related income
item.description: Trump's 927-page financial disclosure report for 2025 totals more than 900 pages detailing holdings in cryptocurrency and stocks.

item.title: More than one-third of employees still work from home, new research shows
item.description: Despite return-to-office policies, the share of employees working from home increased in 2025 from the year before, according to the latest BLS data.

item.title: Buy the UK DIP? Defense stocks lifted by $20 billion spending boost as gilts come under fire
item.description: British defense stocks are marching higher once again after the U.K. confirmed a near-$20 billion military spending boost on Tuesday.

item.title: Iran says it is selling oil at 20% premium as end of U.S. blockade sees 40 million barrels exported
item.description: The ceasefire prompted a surge in crude shipments through the Strait of Hormuz, where traffic had largely ground to a halt during the conflict.

item.title: Gold prices fall further after worst quarter in 13 years as interest rate fears hit bullion
item.description: Gold fell further on Wednesday, with bullion continuing its slide after suffering its worst quarter since 2013.

item.title: Demand for riskier mortgages drops, as their advantages shrink
item.description: The spread between the 30-year fixed-rate mortgage and adjustable-rate loans is narrowing, causing demand for ARMs to weaken.

item.title: New drug data makes this French biotech stock a buy, says Jefferies
item.description: Analyst Faisal Khurshid upgraded the stock to buy from hold.

item.title: Nike results top estimates even as China sales drop 12%; retailer expects $986 million tariff refund
item.description: Nike was expected to report another quarter of declining sales as the sneaker giant struggles to regain strength under its turnaround strategy.

item.title: After a soft Nike quarter, we have a big decision to make on the stock
item.description: The results are unlikely to improve Nike's standing in the portfolio

item.title: Michael Burry says he's shorting Caterpillar for the first time after it nearly doubled in the AI-driven rally of 2026
item.description: "Caterpillar jumped out at me," Burry said. "I have never shorted Caterpillar. It has always done great for me on the long side in the past."

item.title: July is a three-paycheck month. Here's how to make the most of it
item.description: If you're paid biweekly, you get an extra paycheck two months a year. Here's how to put those funds to good use.

item.title: American Airlines brings grab-and-go lounge to New York's JFK
item.description: American Airlines is opening a grab-and-go lounge at New York's JFK

item.title: GOP Rep. Tom Kean says depression diagnosis caused months-long absence from Congress
item.description: New Jersey Rep. Tom Kean said he was diagnosed with depression, explaining a months-long absence from Congress in a competitive district in the midterm elections.

item.title: Big egg producers settle price inflation probe with DOJ for 53 million eggs — and $3.3M
item.description: Cal-Maine Foods, Versova and Hickman's Egg Ranch agreed to settle allegations of price manipulation by the Department of Justice and 17 states.

item.title: FDA lets Philip Morris market Zyn nicotine pouches as less harmful than cigarettes
item.description: The Trump administration has expanded access to nicotine products despite concerns from public health critics about youth use and addiction.

item.title: Trump says Council of Economic Advisers head Pierre Yared is leaving
item.description: The Council of Economic Advisers has provided the president with information and advice on both domestic and international economic policy since 1946.

item.title: Private equity in youth sports draws bipartisan scrutiny in Congress
item.description: Democratic and Republican members of Congress expressed alarm at the trend of private equity investment in youth sports.

item.title: Anthropic launches AI drug discovery program, joining tech giants in betting on healthcare
item.description: Anthropic will start an internal drug discovery program, part of a new push to sell artificial intelligence tools to drugmakers.

item.title: Japan spent $74 billion propping up the yen. Investors say the real battle is with the Fed
item.description: Yen intervention alone is unlikely to reverse losses while wide U.S.-Japan rate differentials keep supporting the dollar, said analysts.
